title: Create durable advanced program packets for AMP56 through AMP62
persona: Executive, admin, security, finance, and customer operations reviewers
steps: Open each AMP56-AMP62 workspace from the Assistant subnav.
Review live source signals, risk assessment, recommendations, artifact, approval route, and rollback controls.
expected: Each workspace persists an AssistantAdvancedProgramPacket row with ampNumber 56 through 62 and a distinct programKey.

sourceSummaryJson includes relevant evidence such as audit events, review examples, playbooks, evidence records, open action queue items, finance risk, invoice intakes, customer/order/shipment signals, and planning health.
recommendationJson, approvalPlanJson, artifactJson, and rollbackPlanJson are specific to the AMP domain.
No extensions, prompts, models, release gates, role grants, crisis rooms, finance postings, customer communications, autonomy policies, goals, or board reports are mutated automatically.

title: Queue human review for extension, evaluation, security, crisis, finance, customer, and executive packets
steps: Create or select a DRAFT AMP56-AMP62 packet.
Add a review note describing the downstream decision under consideration.
expected: The packet status changes to REVIEW_QUEUED and records actionQueueItemId, approvedByUserId, approvedAt, and approvalNote.
An AssistantActionQueueItem is created with an AMP-specific actionKind such as assistant_amp56_review or assistant_amp62_review.
An AssistantAuditEvent states that downstream extensions, releases, security controls, crisis actions, finance postings, customer commitments, and autonomy policies were not changed automatically.

title: Final AMP governance packets preserve release, security, finance, customer, and autonomy controls
steps: Open an AMP56-AMP62 packet with active risks.
Inspect approvalPlanJson, recommendationJson, artifactJson, and rollbackPlanJson.
Confirm the packet is review-only until a human approves downstream execution.
expected: Evaluation packets block prompt/model/workflow releases until release-gate review.
Finance packets preserve ERP-safe exports and no-silent-posting controls.
Customer packets keep portal summaries, commitments, and communications behind review.
Executive cockpit packets require explicit delegation and autonomy approvals before cross-domain work changes.
